description: Rust Codegen Target — Generate a complete Axum server (structs, router, sqlx DB layer, handlers) from TypeScript schemas via typokit build --target rust

userStories.title: Create rust_codegen module with basic struct generation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the foundational rust_codegen module in transform-native that takes a SchemaTypeMap and generates Rust struct files with serde derives, mapping TS primitives to Rust types.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `transform-native/src/rust_codegen/mod.rs` module with public API for Rust code generation
Create `transform-native/src/rust_codegen/structs.rs` that accepts a SchemaTypeMap and produces GeneratedOutput[]
Map TypeScript primitive types to Rust: string → String, number → f64, boolean → bool, Date → chrono::DateTime<Utc>, optional fields → Option<T>
Generate per-entity `.typokit/models/{entity}.rs` files with `#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]`
JSDoc `@table` annotated entities additionally get `#[derive(sqlx::FromRow)]`
Generate `.typokit/models/mod.rs` that re-exports all entity modules
All generated model files use GeneratedOutput with overwrite: true
Register the rust_codegen module in transform-native/src/lib.rs
Typecheck passes (cargo check in transform-native)
userStories.notes: This is the foundational module — all other rust codegen stories depend on it. The module lives at transform-native/src/rust_codegen/ alongside existing generators (openapi_generator.rs, test_stub_generator.rs). Use string-based code generation (not a Rust AST library), matching the pattern used by existing TypeScript codegen.

userStories.title: Add validation annotations and utility type resolution to struct codegen
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the struct generator to map JSDoc validation annotations to validator crate attributes, resolve utility types (Omit/Partial/Pick) to concrete structs, and generate enums from union literals.
acceptanceCriteria: Map @minLength N → #[validate(length(min = N))], @maxLength N → #[validate(length(max = N))]
Map @format email → #[validate(email)], @pattern regex → #[validate(regex(path = ...))]
Map @minimum N → #[validate(range(min = N))], @maximum N → #[validate(range(max = N))]
Structs with any validation annotations get #[derive(Validate)] added
Omit<T, K> generates a concrete struct with the omitted fields removed
Partial<T> generates a concrete struct with all fields wrapped in Option<T>
Pick<T, K> generates a concrete struct with only the picked fields
Union literal types (e.g. 'active' | 'archived') generate Rust enums with #[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")] or per-variant #[serde(rename = "...")]
Generate shared framework types PaginatedResponse<T> and ErrorResponse in .typokit/models/common.rs
Infer integer types from context: @id fields use String (UUID), pagination params use u32, general numbers use f64. Support @integer JSDoc override
Typecheck passes (cargo check in transform-native)
userStories.notes: Extends US-001's struct generator. The validator crate (v0.19) is the standard Rust validation library. Reference the existing type_extractor.rs SchemaTypeMap which already contains validation rules parsed from JSDoc annotations.

userStories.title: Generate Axum router from CompiledRouteTable
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the rust_codegen module to generate an Axum router file that maps each route contract to typed Axum handler registrations with proper extractors.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `transform-native/src/rust_codegen/router.rs` sub-module
Generate `.typokit/router.rs` containing a `pub fn create_router() -> Router<AppState>` function
Each route in CompiledRouteTable maps to axum::routing::{get, post, put, patch, delete} registration
Route path parameters (:id) map to Axum Path<T> extractor in handler signatures
Query types map to Axum Query<T> extractor referencing generated query structs from models
Body types map to Axum Json<T> extractor referencing generated input structs from models
Handler references follow pattern handlers::{entity}::{action} (e.g. handlers::users::list)
Generated router.rs uses GeneratedOutput with overwrite: true
Typecheck passes (cargo check in transform-native)
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001 (references generated model types). Walk the compiled radix tree from route_compiler.rs and emit Router::new().route(...) chains. Axum uses /:id syntax same as Express.

userStories.title: Generate sqlx database layer with CRUD repository and migrations
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the rust_codegen module to generate a PostgreSQL database layer using sqlx, including connection pool setup, CRUD repository functions, and SQL migration files from @table annotations.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `transform-native/src/rust_codegen/database.rs` sub-module
Generate `.typokit/db/mod.rs` with PgPool connection setup and pub async fn connect(database_url: &str) -> PgPool
Generate `.typokit/db/repository.rs` with CRUD functions for each @table entity: find_all_{entity}, find_{entity}_by_id, create_{entity}, update_{entity}, delete_{entity}
find_all functions accept pool, page (u32), page_size (u32) and return sqlx::Result<Vec<Entity>>
find_by_id functions accept pool and id, return sqlx::Result<Option<Entity>>
create functions accept pool and input struct, return sqlx::Result<Entity>
@generated uuid fields use uuid::Uuid::new_v4().to_string() in INSERT, not user-supplied
@generated now fields use chrono::Utc::now() in INSERT
Generate SQL migration files in .typokit/migrations/ with CREATE TABLE statements, proper PostgreSQL column types, and UNIQUE constraints for @unique fields
All generated DB files use GeneratedOutput with overwrite: true
Typecheck passes (cargo check in transform-native)
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001 (references model types for query results). Use sqlx::query_as::<_, Entity>() for runtime-checked queries as default. PostgreSQL only — map string→TEXT, number→DOUBLE PRECISION or BIGINT, boolean→BOOLEAN, Date→TIMESTAMPTZ.

userStories.title: Generate handler stubs wired to repository functions
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the rust_codegen module to generate per-entity handler files with fully working Axum handler functions pre-wired to repository CRUD functions so the server works on first run.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `transform-native/src/rust_codegen/handlers.rs` sub-module
Generate `src/handlers/{entity}.rs` per entity with handler functions matching route signatures
List handlers: extract Query<T>, call repository::find_all_{entity}, return Json(results)
Create handlers: extract Json<T>, call repository::create_{entity}, return Json(created) with StatusCode::CREATED
Get-by-ID handlers: extract Path(id), call repository::find_{entity}_by_id, return 404 AppError::not_found if None
Update handlers: extract Path(id) + Json<T>, call repository::update_{entity}, return 404 if None
Delete handlers: extract Path(id), call repository::delete_{entity}, return StatusCode::NO_CONTENT
All handlers take State(state): State<AppState> for database pool access
Generate src/handlers/mod.rs with pub mod declarations for all entity handler modules
Handler entity files use GeneratedOutput with overwrite: false (never overwrite user code)
src/handlers/mod.rs uses GeneratedOutput with overwrite: true (stays in sync with schema)
Typecheck passes (cargo check in transform-native)
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001 (model types), US-003 (router references these handlers), US-004 (handlers call repository functions). Handlers follow the same pattern as TypeScript example-todo-server: extract → validate → call service/repo → respond.

userStories.title: Generate service and middleware stubs
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the codegen to generate service-layer and middleware stub files following the same functional pattern as the TypeScript example apps, so user code has a clear place to live.
acceptanceCriteria: Generate `src/services/mod.rs` with pub mod declarations for all entity service modules (overwrite: true)
Generate `src/services/{entity}.rs` per entity with placeholder service functions matching CRUD patterns (overwrite: false)
Service stubs import entity model types and include function signatures for list, get_by_id, create, update, delete
Generate `src/middleware/mod.rs` with pub mod auth declaration (overwrite: true)
Generate `src/middleware/auth.rs` stub with an Axum middleware::from_fn pattern for auth checking (overwrite: false)
Service and middleware stubs compile (no unresolved imports or type errors in generated code)
Typecheck passes (cargo check in transform-native)
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001 (model types for service signatures). Service stubs follow the TypeScript pattern: exported named functions (not classes). Middleware stub uses tower/axum middleware pattern with async fn auth(req, next) -> Response.

userStories.title: Generate project scaffold (Cargo.toml, main.rs, lib.rs, app.rs, error.rs)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the codegen to generate the complete Rust project scaffold so that cargo build works immediately after all codegen files are emitted.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `transform-native/src/rust_codegen/project.rs` sub-module

Generate Cargo.toml with dependencies: axum 0.8, tokio 1 (full), serde 1 (derive), serde_json 1, validator 0.19 (derive), chrono 0.4 (serde), uuid 1, sqlx 0.8 (runtime-tokio, postgres), tower-http 0.6 (cors, trace), tracing, tracing-subscriber, dotenvy
Cargo.toml uses edition = '2024' and has correct [package] metadata
Generate src/main.rs with: #[tokio::main], dotenvy::dotenv().ok(), database pool from DATABASE_URL env var, router creation, tracing subscriber init, axum::serve on 0.0.0.0:3000
Generate src/lib.rs with #[path] attributes bridging .typokit/ modules (models, router, db, error, app) plus pub mod handlers/services/middleware
Generate .typokit/app.rs with #[derive(Clone)] pub struct AppState { pub pool: PgPool }
Generate .typokit/error.rs with AppError enum (NotFound, BadRequest, Unauthorized, Forbidden, Conflict, Internal) implementing axum::response::IntoResponse with correct HTTP status codes
Cargo.toml, src/main.rs, src/lib.rs use overwrite: true
.typokit/app.rs and .typokit/error.rs use overwrite: true
Typecheck passes (cargo check in transform-native)
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001 through US-006 (needs to know all modules and dependencies). The scaffold ties everything together. src/lib.rs uses #[path] attributes to bridge .typokit/ into the Rust module system — same .typokit/ convention as TypeScript projects.

userStories.title: Add --target rust CLI flag to typokit build
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to run 'typokit build --target rust' to invoke the Rust codegen pipeline from the CLI, hooking into the existing tapable emit phase.
acceptanceCriteria: Add --target option to the build command in packages/cli with values: 'typescript' (default), 'rust'
Add --db option accepted when --target rust is used, defaulting to 'sqlx'
Add --out option for specifying output directory (defaults to current directory)
When --target rust, invoke rust_codegen generate function in emit phase with SchemaTypeMap and CompiledRouteTable
When --target typescript (or no flag), existing TypeScript codegen runs as before (no regression)
CLI --help output documents the new --target, --db, and --out flags
Show error message if --target rust is used with --db value other than 'sqlx'
Content-hash caching (.typokit/.cache-hash) applies to Rust codegen
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001 through US-007. The CLI hooks into tapable build pipeline: afterTypeParse + afterRouteTable + emit. The rust_codegen module is called from the napi-rs bridge.

userStories.title: Create example-todo-server-axum reference application
userStories.description: As a developer evaluating TypoKit, I want a complete reference application in packages/example-todo-server-axum/ demonstrating the Rust codegen target end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: Create packages/example-todo-server-axum/ directory as a new monorepo package
Package reuses @typokit/example-todo-schema as the TypeScript type source
.typokit/ directory contains generated Rust files (checked in for demonstration)
src/handlers/ contains user-written Rust handlers implementing full todo and users API
src/services/ contains service-layer functions following the same functional pattern as TypeScript example
API endpoints match TypeScript example-todo-server: same paths, HTTP methods, request/response shapes
README.md documents: prerequisites (rustup, cargo, PostgreSQL), build steps, run steps, API examples
Cargo.toml metadata includes publish = false
The project compiles with cargo build
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-008 (needs CLI --target rust). This is the showpiece: TS schema → typokit build --target rust → cargo run → working Axum API. @typokit/client works against this server identically to the TypeScript server.

userStories.title: Update documentation for Rust codegen target
userStories.description: As a developer or contributor, I want project documentation to reflect the new Rust codegen target including architecture, type mappings, CLI usage, and file structure.
acceptanceCriteria: typokit-arch.md has new 'Rust Codegen Target' section explaining architecture and adapter pattern
Architecture doc includes Axum row in server adapter compatibility matrix
Document --target rust CLI flag with usage examples
AGENTS.md scope list updated to include rust_codegen modules
Document generated file structure: .typokit/ (regen) vs src/ (user-owned) for Rust target
Document TypeScript → Rust type mapping table
Document JSDoc annotation → Rust validator/sqlx mapping table
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-009. Documentation-only story — no code changes except markdown files.
